    29. canAffectFirstTrueCell(short, short, Grid.ValueFormat)

    33. printGrid()

Class Grid

java.lang.Object
com.github.mrgarbagegamer.Grid
Direct Known Subclasses:
Grid13, Grid22, Grid35
public abstract class Grid extends Object
A structure that represents the core hexagonal grid for a "Lights Out" style puzzle.

This abstract class provides a high-performance, bitmask-based representation of a 109-cell hexagonal grid. It is designed for a specific variant of the puzzle where clicking a cell toggles the state of its adjacent cells only, not the cell itself. The primary goal is to turn off all the lights on the grid.

Architecture Role

As the foundational data structure, Grid defines the puzzle's state and core operations. It is extended by concrete implementations such as Grid13, Grid22, and Grid35, which provide specific initial puzzle configurations.

Worker threads ("monkeys") interact with cloned instances of this class to test solutions. In contrast, generators perform lighter, more frequent checks and have limited direct interaction with this class. This separation ensures that optimizations to this class primarily benefit the state-intensive work of the monkeys.

Performance Characteristics

The grid state is stored in a long[2] array, treated as a 128-bit bitmask to represent the 109 cells. This approach minimizes memory footprint and allows for extremely fast state manipulation using bitwise operations. Adjacency information is pre-computed into ADJACENCY_MASKS, enabling O(1) complexity for the critical click operation.

Several alternatives were evaluated:

  • BitSet: Incurs unacceptable overhead from object headers and indirect memory access compared to a primitive array.
  • Panama/Vector API: Showed promise for 128-bit operations but suffered from excessive memory allocation (4+ allocations per lanewise operation in tested JDK versions), making it unsuitable for the hot path.
The primitive long[] array was ultimately chosen as the most performant solution on the modern JVM, despite the complexity of managing two separate longs.

Future Optimizations

Further performance gains are likely limited by the JVM itself. Potential avenues for exploration include off-heap memory storage or the availability of true 128-bit primitives in a future Java version (e.g., via Project Valhalla), which would simplify the bitmask logic to a single long.

Static Initialization

A key performance feature is the extensive use of a static initializer block. This block runs only once when the class is loaded and pre-computes several critical data structures:

  • ADJACENCY_MASKS: Bitmasks for every cell, allowing a click to be a simple XOR operation.
  • adjacencyArray: A legacy structure providing adjacent cell indices for algorithms that require iteration.
  • ADJACENCY_CACHE: A boolean matrix for O(1) adjacency checks between any two cells.
  • PACKED_TO_INDEX_CACHE: A lookup table for fast conversion from human-readable Grid.ValueFormat.PackedInt to the internal Grid.ValueFormat.Index.
This pre-computation offloads complex calculations from the performance-critical runtime paths.

Thread Safety

This class is not thread-safe. Each instance is designed to be used by a single thread. State-modifying methods like click(short) are unsynchronized to maximize performance. Static members are effectively immutable after initialization and are safe to be shared across threads.

Since:
2025.03 - Initial Creation
Performance Characteristics:
Critical operations like click(short) and state checks are O(1) due to extensive pre-computation. The majority of computational complexity is handled once in a static initializer.
Threading Model:
Not thread-safe. Instances of Grid must not be shared between threads without external synchronization.
Algorithm Details:
Uses a bitmask (long[2]) to represent the grid state. Clicks are performed using pre-computed adjacency masks and bitwise XOR operations. Adjacency lookups and format conversions are accelerated by statically initialized caches.

    Defines the different formats used to represent a cell's location on the grid.

    Description
    private static final boolean[][]
    ADJACENCY_CACHE
    A pre-computed boolean matrix for O(1) adjacency checks between any two cells.

    ADJACENCY_MASKS
    Pre-computed bitmasks representing the result of clicking each cell.

    A pre-computed table storing the adjacent cells for each cell as an array of indices.

    The number of columns in the even-indexed rows of the grid (rows 0, 2, 4, 6).

    A cached index of the first "on" (true) cell in the grid, in Grid.ValueFormat.Index format.

    trueCellsCount

  • Field Details

    • NUM_ROWS

      public static final int NUM_ROWS
      The number of rows in the hexagonal grid.
      Since:
      2025.03 - Grid Definition
      See Also:
      • EVEN_NUM_COLS
      • NUM_CELLS
      • ODD_NUM_COLS
      • ROW_OFFSETS
      • Constant Field Values
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe as a static final constant.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes as a primitive int.

    • ODD_NUM_COLS

    • EVEN_NUM_COLS

      public static final int EVEN_NUM_COLS
      The number of columns in the even-indexed rows of the grid (rows 0, 2, 4, 6).
      Since:
      2025.03 - Grid Definition
      See Also:
      • NUM_CELLS
      • NUM_ROWS
      • ODD_NUM_COLS
      • ROW_OFFSETS

    • ROW_OFFSETS

      public static final short[] ROW_OFFSETS
      Pre-computed offsets for the starting index of each row in the flattened grid.

      This array is used to accelerate the conversion from Grid.ValueFormat.PackedInt to Grid.ValueFormat.Index by providing an O(1) lookup for the base index of any given row. For example, ROW_OFFSETS[2] gives the index of the first cell in row 2.

      Since:
      2025.06 - BitSet Grid State
      See Also:
      • EVEN_NUM_COLS
      • NUM_ROWS
      • ODD_NUM_COLS
      • computePackedToIndex(short)
      • Grid.ValueFormat
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe as a static final constant.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 14 bytes (7 shorts) as a short[].

    • NUM_CELLS

      public static final int NUM_CELLS
      The total number of cells in the grid.

      Calculated from the number of rows and their respective column counts: (4 rows × 16 cols) + (3 rows × 15 cols) = 109 total cells. This constant is used to define the size of arrays and loop bounds throughout the class.

      Since:
      2025.04 - Static Block Initialization
      See Also:
      • gridState
      • NUM_ROWS
      • ROW_OFFSETS
      • trueCellsCount
      • Constant Field Values
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe as a static final constant.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes as a primitive int.

    • gridState

      protected final long[] gridState
      The state of the grid, represented as a 128-bit bitmask split across two long values.

      A 1 at a given bit position indicates the cell is true while a 0 indicates it is false The first long (gridState[0]) stores the state for cells 0-63, and the second (gridState[1]) stores the state for cells 64-108.

      This primitive array representation was chosen over BitSet and the Vector API to eliminate object allocation overhead and ensure cache-friendliness in the hot path. While Java's lack of a 128-bit primitive adds complexity, this approach provides the highest performance on the modern JVM for the solver's workload.

      Since:
      2025.07 - Bitmasked Grid State
      See Also:
      • clearBit(int)
      • getBit(int)
      • getGridState()
      • printGrid()
      • setBit(int)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) accesses and modifications using bitwise operations.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe. Instances of Grid must not be shared between threads without external synchronization.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 16 bytes (2 longs) per instance.

    • trueCellsCount

      protected int trueCellsCount
      A cached count of the number of "on" (true) cells in the grid.

      This value is updated lazily. It is only recalculated by methods like getTrueCount() or findFirstTrueCell(ValueFormat) when the recalculationNeeded flag is true. This makes isSolved() checks instantaneous in most cases.

      Since:
      2025.07 - Bitmasked Grid State
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) accesses and O(1) recalculations.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe. Instances of Grid must not be shared between threads without external synchronization.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes as a primitive int.

    • firstTrueCell

      protected short firstTrueCell
      A cached index of the first "on" (true) cell in the grid, in Grid.ValueFormat.Index format.

      This value is critical for pruning the search space in the CombinationGeneratorTask, as any valid solution must interact with the first true cell. It is updated lazily whenever findFirstTrueCell() is called and recalculationNeeded is true. A value of -1 indicates no cells are true.

      Since:
      2025.07 - First True Cell Caching
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access and O(1) recalculations.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe. Instances of Grid must not be shared between threads without external synchronization.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 2 bytes as a primitive short.

    • recalculationNeeded

      protected boolean recalculationNeeded
      A dirty flag indicating that the grid state has been modified and cached values are stale.

      This flag is set to true by any click operation. It signals to accessor methods like getTrueCount() and findFirstTrueCell() that they must recompute the trueCellsCount and firstTrueCell caches before returning a value. It is reset to false after the caches are updated.

      Since:
      2025.07 - Bitmasked Grid State
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe. Instances of Grid must not be shared between threads without external synchronization.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 1 byte as a primitive boolean.

    • ADJACENCY_MASKS

      private static final long[][] ADJACENCY_MASKS
      Pre-computed bitmasks representing the result of clicking each cell.

      Each entry ADJACENCY_MASKS[i] is a long[2] bitmask where the set bits correspond to the cells adjacent to cell i. This is the core optimization that makes the click operation an O(1) bitwise XOR, as it eliminates the need for runtime adjacency lookups.

      This array is populated once in a static initializer block when the class is loaded. Though a click could only affect cells in one of the two longs, we store the full long[2] for simplicity and to avoid conditional logic in the hot path.

      Since:
      2025.04 - Static Block Initialization
      See Also:
      • NUM_CELLS
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe as a static final constant after class initialization.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of ~NUM_CELLS × 2 × 8 bytes as a long[][].

    • adjacencyArray

      private static final short[][] adjacencyArray
      A pre-computed table storing the adjacent cells for each cell as an array of indices.

      Each entry adjacencyArray[i] contains a short[] listing the neighbors of cell i in Grid.ValueFormat.Index format. This structure provides O(1) lookups for the findAdjacents(short, ValueFormat, ValueFormat) method and serves as a legacy alternative to the bitmask-based approach, used in parts of the code that require iterating over neighbors.

      Since:
      2025.05 - Adjacency Storage Optimization
      See Also:
      • NUM_CELLS
      • computeAdjacents(short, ValueFormat, ValueFormat)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) lookup time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe as a static final constant after class initialization.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint as a short[][].

    • ADJACENCY_CACHE

      private static final boolean[][] ADJACENCY_CACHE
      A pre-computed boolean matrix for O(1) adjacency checks between any two cells.

      An entry ADJACENCY_CACHE[i][j] is true if cell i and cell j are adjacent. This cache is used by areAdjacent(short, short) to provide near-instantaneous lookups. While not used in most hot paths, it is a useful utility for specific algorithmic checks.

      Since:
      2025.06 - O(1) Adjacency Check
      See Also:
      • NUM_CELLS
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) lookup time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe as a static final constant after class initialization.

    • PACKED_TO_INDEX_CACHE

      private static final short[] PACKED_TO_INDEX_CACHE
      A pre-computed lookup table to accelerate conversion from Grid.ValueFormat.PackedInt to Grid.ValueFormat.Index.

      The index of the array corresponds to a PackedInt value, and the element at that index is the corresponding Index value. This provides an O(1) conversion, avoiding the arithmetic typically required.

      Since:
      2025.06 - PackedInt to Index Precomputation
      See Also:
      • NUM_CELLS
      • computePackedToIndex(short)
      • Grid.ValueFormat
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) lookup time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe as a static final constant after class initialization.

  • Constructor Details

    • Grid

      public Grid()
      Constructs a new Grid instance and initializes it to a specific starting state.

      As Grid is an abstract class, this constructor is implicitly called by concrete subclass constructors (e.g., Grid13, Grid22, Grid35). It ensures that the grid is properly set up by invoking the abstract initialize() method, which subclasses must implement to define their unique initial puzzle configurations.

      Since:
      2025.03 - Abstract Grid Introduction
      See Also:
      • initialize()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) complexity for construction, plus the complexity of initialize().
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; each instance is independent.

  • Method Details

    • computeAdjacents

      public static ShortList computeAdjacents(short cell, Grid.ValueFormat inputFormat, Grid.ValueFormat outputFormat)
      Computes the hexagonally adjacent cells for a given cell, supporting flexible input and output formats.

      This method is primarily used during the static initialization block to build the adjacencyArray and ADJACENCY_MASKS. It handles the complex adjacency rules of a hexagonal grid, which vary based on a cell's row and column parity, unlike a simple rectangular grid.

      Algorithm Details

      Adjacency calculations are performed using the Grid.ValueFormat.PackedInt format internally due to its straightforward arithmetic for determining relative positions. The algorithm dynamically adds potential neighbors based on the cell's row parity (even or odd).

      • Even Rows: Cells (row-1, col-1), (row-1, col), (row, col-1), (row, col+1), (row+1, col-1), (row+1, col).
      • Odd Rows: Cells (row-1, col), (row-1, col+1), (row, col-1), (row, col+1), (row+1, col), (row+1, col+1).

      After identifying potential neighbors, the method filters out any cells that fall outside the grid boundaries.

      Performance Considerations

      The method's complexity is O(1) because the number of potential neighbors is small and constant (at most 6). It uses FastUtil's ShortArrayList to efficiently store results without boxing overhead. While format conversions add minor overhead, this is acceptable as the method's primary use case is during the one-time static initialization.

      Parameters:
      cell - The cell for which to find adjacents, in the specified inputFormat.
      inputFormat - The Grid.ValueFormat of the input cell.
      outputFormat - The Grid.ValueFormat in which the adjacent cells should be returned.
      Returns:
      A ShortList of adjacent cells in the specified outputFormat.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if Grid.ValueFormat.Bitmask is used for inputFormat or outputFormat, as it is not suitable for single-cell representation.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Format Support
      See Also:
      • findAdjacents(short, ValueFormat, ValueFormat)
      • ShortArrayList(int)
      • ShortList
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) complexity due to constant number of potential neighbors.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; does not modify instance state.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a new ShortArrayList of capacity 6 for the result.

    • computeAdjacents

      public static ShortList computeAdjacents(short cell, Grid.ValueFormat format)
      Convenience overload for computeAdjacents(short, ValueFormat, ValueFormat) that assumes the input and output formats are the same.
      Parameters:
      cell - The cell for which to find adjacents.
      format - The Grid.ValueFormat for both the input cell and the output adjacent cells.
      Returns:
      A ShortList of adjacent cells in the specified format.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if Grid.ValueFormat.Bitmask is used.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Format Support
      See Also:
      • computeAdjacents(short, ValueFormat, ValueFormat)
      Performance Characteristics:
      Delegates to the main implementation; O(1) complexity.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; delegates to a thread-safe method.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a new ShortArrayList with a capacity of 6 for the result.

    • computeAdjacents

      public static ShortList computeAdjacents(short cell)
      Convenience overload for computeAdjacents(short, ValueFormat) that assumes Grid.ValueFormat.Index for both input and output.
      Parameters:
      cell - The cell for which to find adjacents, in Grid.ValueFormat.Index format.
      Returns:
      A ShortList of adjacent cells in Grid.ValueFormat.Index format.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if Grid.ValueFormat.Bitmask is implicitly used (though unlikely with this overload).
      Since:
      2025.07 - Format Support
      See Also:
      • computeAdjacents(short, ValueFormat, ValueFormat)
      Performance Characteristics:
      Delegates to the main implementation; O(1) complexity.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; delegates to a thread-safe method.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a new ShortArrayList with a capacity of 6 for the result.

    • findAdjacents

      public static short[] findAdjacents(short cell, Grid.ValueFormat inputFormat, Grid.ValueFormat outputFormat)
      Finds the hexagonally adjacent cells for a given cell, supporting flexible input and output formats.

      This method retrieves pre-computed adjacency data from the adjacencyArray. It handles format conversions as needed, making it versatile for different use cases, such as debugging, logging, or specific algorithmic checks.

      Performance Considerations

      The core lookup from adjacencyArray is an O(1) operation. However, if the outputFormat is not Grid.ValueFormat.Index, the method incurs additional overhead for converting each adjacent cell's format. This makes the method's complexity O(k) where k is the number of adjacent cells (at most 6). The presence of switch statements also limits JIT inlining opportunities.

      Though specialized caches for each format could solve these problems, since this method is not on the critical performance path, the overhead of format conversion and branching is deemed acceptable. For hot paths requiring adjacency information, direct access to ADJACENCY_MASKS or adjacencyArray in Grid.ValueFormat.Index is preferred.

      Parameters:
      cell - The cell for which to find adjacents.
      inputFormat - The Grid.ValueFormat of the input cell.
      outputFormat - The Grid.ValueFormat in which the adjacent cells should be returned.
      Returns:
      A short[] of adjacent cells in the specified outputFormat.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if Grid.ValueFormat.Bitmask is used for inputFormat or outputFormat.
      ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException - if the input cell is out of bounds for the specified inputFormat.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Format Support
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for Grid.ValueFormat.Index output, otherwise O(k) where k is the number of adjacent cells (max 6).
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; accesses immutable, pre-computed static data.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a new short[] for the result only if format conversion is necessary.

    • findAdjacents

      public static short[] findAdjacents(short cell, Grid.ValueFormat format)
      Convenience overload for findAdjacents(short, ValueFormat, ValueFormat) that assumes the input and output formats are the same.
      Parameters:
      cell - The cell for which to find adjacents.
      format - The Grid.ValueFormat for both the input cell and the output adjacent cells.
      Returns:
      A short[] of adjacent cells in the specified format.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if Grid.ValueFormat.Bitmask is used.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Format Support
      See Also:
      • findAdjacents(short, ValueFormat, ValueFormat)
      Performance Characteristics:
      Delegates to the main implementation; O(k) complexity where k is the number of adjacent cells.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; delegates to a thread-safe method.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a new short[] for the result only if format conversion is necessary.

    • findAdjacents

      public static short[] findAdjacents(short cell)
      Convenience overload for findAdjacents(short, ValueFormat) that assumes Grid.ValueFormat.Index for both input and output.
      Parameters:
      cell - The cell for which to find adjacents, in Grid.ValueFormat.Index format.
      Returns:
      A short[] of adjacent cells in Grid.ValueFormat.Index format.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Format Support
      See Also:
      • findAdjacents(short, ValueFormat, ValueFormat)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) complexity as it directly returns a reference to a pre-computed array.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; delegates to a thread-safe method.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate; returns a reference to an existing short[].

    • computePackedToIndex

      private static short computePackedToIndex(short packed)
      Computes the conversion from Grid.ValueFormat.PackedInt to Grid.ValueFormat.Index format.

      This method performs the necessary arithmetic to determine the correct Index for a given PackedInt value, taking into account the varying row lengths of the hexagonal grid. It extracts the row and column from the PackedInt and uses the ROW_OFFSETS array to calculate the flattened index.

      This method is primarily used during static initialization to populate the PACKED_TO_INDEX_CACHE. It does not perform bounds checking, assuming valid input during its intended use.

      Parameters:
      packed - The cell in Grid.ValueFormat.PackedInt format.
      Returns:
      The cell in Grid.ValueFormat.Index format.
      Since:
      2025.06 - Grid.ValueFormat.PackedInt to Grid.ValueFormat.Index Precomputation
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) complexity due to direct arithmetic and a single array lookup.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; does not modify instance state.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • packedToIndex

      public static final short packedToIndex(short packed)
      Converts a cell from Grid.ValueFormat.PackedInt to Grid.ValueFormat.Index format using a pre-computed cache.

      This method leverages the PACKED_TO_INDEX_CACHE for O(1) lookups. If a value is not found in the cache (which should only happen for packed = 0 during initial static setup), it is computed on-the-fly using computePackedToIndex(short).

      Optimization Rationale

      The cache is partially populated during static initialization to avoid a full pre-computation of all possible PackedInt values, which would increase startup time for a conversion that is not frequently used in performance-critical paths. A TODO comment indicates a potential future optimization to fully pre-compute the cache, which would further reduce method size and improve JIT inlining chances.

      Parameters:
      packed - The cell in Grid.ValueFormat.PackedInt format.
      Returns:
      The cell in Grid.ValueFormat.Index format.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the input packed value is out of bounds.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Format Support
      See Also:
      • PACKED_TO_INDEX_CACHE
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) lookup.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; accesses immutable, pre-computed static data.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • indexToPacked

      public static final short indexToPacked(short index)
      Converts a cell from Grid.ValueFormat.Index to Grid.ValueFormat.PackedInt format.

      This method uses a series of conditional checks to determine the correct row and column for the given Index, leveraging the grid's alternating row lengths. It constructs the PackedInt value by combining the calculated row and column.

      Performance Considerations

      This method is O(1) in complexity due to the fixed number of comparisons. It is declared final to encourage JIT inlining, as it is a pure function with no side effects. While a cache could be implemented (similar to packedToIndex(short)), it is not deemed necessary given that this conversion is not frequently called in performance-critical paths.

      Parameters:
      index - The cell in Grid.ValueFormat.Index format.
      Returns:
      The cell in Grid.ValueFormat.PackedInt format.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the input index is out of bounds (0-108).
      Since:
      2025.07 - Format Support
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) comparisons and computation.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; does not modify instance state.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • initialize

      abstract void initialize()
      Initializes the Grid instance to a specific starting state.

      This abstract method must be implemented by concrete subclasses to define their unique initial puzzle configurations. It is called by the constructor during instance creation, ensuring the grid state is properly set up from the outset.

      Since:
      2025.03 - Abstract Grid Introduction
      See Also:
      • Grid13
      • Grid22
      • Grid35
      Performance Characteristics:
      Implementation-dependent.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; this method modifies the instance's gridState.

    • setBit

      protected void setBit(int index)
      Sets the bit at the specified index in the grid state to true.

      This is a low-level helper method primarily used during grid initialization. It updates the bitmask and increments trueCellsCount if the bit was previously false. For performance-critical click operations, click(short[]) is used instead, which leverages pre-computed ADJACENCY_MASKS.

      Parameters:
      index - The index of the bit to set (0-108).
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - (Implicitly) if the index is out of bounds for the long[] array.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Bitmasked Grid State
      See Also:
      • clearBit(int)
      • getBit(int)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) bitwise operation.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; modifies gridState and trueCellsCount.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • clearBit

      protected void clearBit(int index)
      Clears the bit at the specified index in the grid state to false.

      This is a low-level helper method, analogous to setBit(int). It updates the bitmask and decrements trueCellsCount if the bit was previously true. It is not used in performance-critical paths, which prefer click(short[]).

      Parameters:
      index - The index of the bit to clear (0-108).
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - (Implicitly) if the index is out of bounds for the long[] array.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Bitmasked Grid State
      See Also:
      • getBit(int)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) bitwise operation.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; modifies gridState and trueCellsCount.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • getBit

      protected boolean getBit(int index)
      Checks the state of the bit at the specified index in the grid state.

      This method is used to determine if a specific cell is true (on) or false (off). It is utilized internally for operations like printGrid() or when iterating to find true cells.

      Parameters:
      index - The index of the bit to check (0-108).
      Returns:
      true if the bit is set (cell is "on"), false otherwise.
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - (Implicitly) if the index is out of bounds for the long[] array.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Bitmasked Grid State
      See Also:
      • clearBit(int)
      • setBit(int)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) bitwise operation.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; reads potentially mutable gridState.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • findTrueCells

      public short[] findTrueCells(Grid.ValueFormat format)
      Scans the grid bitmask and returns an array of all true cells in the requested format.

      This method is used by generators and monkeys to extract the current state of the grid for pruning or processing. It iterates through the grid's bits and collects the indices of all set bits.

      Algorithm Details

      The method iterates from 0 to 109-1, checking each bit's state using getBit(int). It populates a short[] which is pre-sized based on trueCellsCount to avoid dynamic resizing. The iteration stops early once all expected true cells are found.

      Performance Considerations

      This method has a worst-case complexity of O(NUM_CELLS). While a more optimized approach could use Long.numberOfTrailingZeros(long) to find set bits directly, this method is not on a critical performance path (as it's mainly used for initial state extraction or debugging) and the current implementation is clear and sufficient. The trueCellsCount is assumed to be accurately maintained by setBit(int) and clearBit(int).

      Parameters:
      format - The desired output format (Grid.ValueFormat.Index or Grid.ValueFormat.PackedInt).
      Returns:
      A short[] of true cells in the specified format.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if Grid.ValueFormat.Bitmask is provided, as it is not suitable for representing individual cells.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Format Support
      See Also:
      • findFirstTrueCell(ValueFormat)
      • getGridState()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(NUM_CELLS) in the worst case.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; reads the mutable gridState.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a new short[] for the result.

    • findTrueCells

      public final short[] findTrueCells()
      Scans the grid bitmask and returns an array of all true cells in Index format.

      This is a convenience overload of findTrueCells(ValueFormat) that defaults to the Grid.ValueFormat.Index format, providing a simpler API for the most common use case.

      Returns:
      A short[] of true cells in Grid.ValueFormat.Index format.
      Since:
      2025.04 - Adjacency Optimizations
      See Also:
      • findTrueCells(ValueFormat)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(NUM_CELLS) in the worst case.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; reads mutable state.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a new short[] for the result.

    • findFirstTrueCell

      public short findFirstTrueCell(Grid.ValueFormat format)
      Scans the grid and returns the first true cell in the requested format.

      This method is crucial for optimizing the solution search. A core property of the puzzle is that any valid solution must interact with the first true cell in the initial grid state. This method provides a fast way to identify that cell, enabling early pruning of invalid combinations.

      Algorithm Details

      The method employs highly optimized Long.numberOfTrailingZeros(long) and Long.bitCount(long) intrinsics to efficiently determine the first true bit and the total count of true bits in the long[2] gridState.

      Performance Considerations

      This method implements a lazy recalculation strategy for firstTrueCell and trueCellsCount. These values are only recomputed if the recalculationNeeded flag is true, ensuring O(1) performance in most cases. If no true cells exist, it short-circuits to return -1 immediately.

      Parameters:
      format - The desired output format (Grid.ValueFormat.Index or Grid.ValueFormat.PackedInt).
      Returns:
      The first true cell in the specified format, or -1 if no true cell is found.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if Grid.ValueFormat.Bitmask is used, as it is not suitable for single-cell representation.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Format Support
      See Also:
      • click(short[])
      • findTrueCells(ValueFormat)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) complexity due to lazy evaluation and highly optimized intrinsics.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe due to lazy evaluation of mutable cached fields.
      Algorithm Details:
      Uses Long.numberOfTrailingZeros(long) and Long.bitCount(long) intrinsics.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • findFirstTrueCell

      public final short findFirstTrueCell()
      Scans the grid and returns the first true cell in Index format.

      This is a convenience overload of findFirstTrueCell(ValueFormat) that strips the format conversion, providing a simpler API for the most common use case where the generator requires the index.

      Returns:
      The first true cell in Grid.ValueFormat.Index format, or -1 if no true cell is found.
      Since:
      2025.04 - Adjacency Optimizations
      See Also:
      • findTrueCells()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) complexity due to lazy evaluation and optimized intrinsics.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe due to lazy evaluation of mutable cached fields.
      Algorithm Details:
      Uses Long.numberOfTrailingZeros(long) and Long.bitCount(long) intrinsics.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • click

      public void click(short cell, Grid.ValueFormat format)
      Simulates a click on the Grid at the specified cell, supporting Grid.ValueFormat.Index or Grid.ValueFormat.PackedInt formats.

      A click toggles the state of its adjacent cells (excluding itself). This operation is performed by XORing the grid state with a pre-computed adjacency mask corresponding to the clicked cell.

      The recalculationNeeded flag is set to true to ensure that subsequent calls to findFirstTrueCell(ValueFormat) or getTrueCount() will recompute the cached firstTrueCell and trueCellsCount values. This lazy evaluation avoids unnecessary recalculations on every click.

      Performance Considerations

      This method achieves O(1) complexity due to the direct bitwise XOR operations on the long[2] gridState using pre-computed masks. While the grid spans 109 cells, requiring two long values for the bitmask, the implementation avoids conditional branching based on cell position for simplicity and consistent performance.

      Parameters:
      cell - The cell to click, in the specified format.
      format - The Grid.ValueFormat of the input cell (Index or PackedInt).
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if Grid.ValueFormat.Bitmask is used, as it is not suitable for single-cell representation.
      ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException - if the cell is out of bounds (implicitly checked by array accesses).
      Since:
      2025.07 - Format Support
      See Also:
      • click(short[])
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) complexity due to bitwise operations and pre-computed masks.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; modifies the instance's gridState.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • click

      public final void click(short cell)
      Deprecated.
      As of 2025.07, replaced by click(short[]) for bulk operations. Single-click operations are no longer on the hot path.
      Simulates a click on the Grid at the specified cell, assuming Grid.ValueFormat.Index format.

      This is a highly optimized, final method designed for performance-critical paths. It directly applies the pre-computed adjacency mask to the grid state using bitwise XOR.

      The recalculationNeeded flag is set to true to trigger lazy recomputation of firstTrueCell and trueCellsCount.

      Performance Considerations

      This method is O(1) in complexity. It is declared final to encourage JIT inlining and avoids any format-checking overhead by requiring the Grid.ValueFormat.Index format. This method has largely been superseded by click(short[]) for bulk operations in TestClickCombination, as single-click operations are no longer the primary hot path.

      Parameters:
      cell - The cell to click, in Grid.ValueFormat.Index format (0-108).
      Throws:
      ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException - if the cell is out of bounds (implicitly checked by array accesses).
      Since:
      2025.07 - Inlining Improvements
      See Also:
      • areAdjacent(short, short, ValueFormat)
      • click(short, ValueFormat)
      • computeAdjacents(short, ValueFormat, ValueFormat)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) retrieval and bitwise operations.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; modifies the instance's gridState.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • click

      public final void click(short row, short col)
      Simulates a click on the Grid at the specified row and column.

      This is a convenience final method that converts the row and column into a Grid.ValueFormat.PackedInt value, then converts it to Grid.ValueFormat.Index format, before delegating to the core click(short) method.

      Parameters:
      row - The row of the cell to click.
      col - The column of the cell to click.
      Throws:
      ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException - if the cell is out of bounds (implicitly checked by array accesses).
      Since:
      2025.03 - Initial Creation
      See Also:
      • click(short, ValueFormat)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) complexity.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; modifies the instance's gridState.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • click

      public void click(long[] bitmask)
      Applies a pre-computed bitmask to the grid state.

      This method provides a direct way to modify the grid state by XORing it with an external bitmask. It is intended for advanced scenarios where the caller has already calculated the cumulative effect of one or more clicks as a bitmask.

      The recalculationNeeded flag is set to true to trigger lazy recomputation of firstTrueCell and trueCellsCount.

      Parameters:
      bitmask - The bitmask (a long[2] array) representing the changes to apply to the grid.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the bitmask array is not of length 2.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Click Format Support
      See Also:
      • click(short[])
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) bitwise operations.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; modifies the instance's gridState.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • click

      public final void click(short[] cells)
      Simulates clicks on multiple cells in the Grid.

      This method efficiently processes an array of cells (in Grid.ValueFormat.Index format) by iteratively applying their corresponding ADJACENCY_MASKS to the grid state using bitwise XOR operations. This is the primary method for applying click combinations in bulk, particularly within monkeys.

      The recalculationNeeded flag is set to true once after all clicks are applied, ensuring lazy recomputation of firstTrueCell and trueCellsCount.

      Performance Considerations

      This method is a hot-path operation with O(cells.length) complexity. It is declared final to encourage JIT inlining and assumes valid input to minimize branching and checks within the loop. We avoid unrolling the loop to delegate the responsibility to the JIT compiler. Vectorization is also not applicable here, due to the non-predictable array accesses.

      Parameters:
      cells - An array of cells (in Grid.ValueFormat.Index format) to click.
      Throws:
      ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException - if any cell in the array is out of bounds.
      NullPointerException - if the cells array is null.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Bulk Clicks
      See Also:
      • click(short)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(cells.length) for iterating over cells.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; modifies the instance's gridState.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • findFirstTrueAdjacents

      public short[] findFirstTrueAdjacents(Grid.ValueFormat format)
      Returns an array of adjacent cells to the first true cell in the requested format.

      This method leverages the puzzle property that any solution must interact with the first true cell. It provides the generator with the adjacent cells of this critical point, enabling focused exploration of the search space.

      Bitmask formats are not supported for this method. While it's technically possible to represent small sets of values as bitmasks (e.g., a long[2] or a short[7]), the overhead of conversion and potential for wasted space makes it impractical for this context.

      Performance Considerations

      The operation is O(1) because it involves a simple lookup of the first true cell and then retrieving its pre-computed adjacents. While caching the result could be considered, the small, fixed size of the adjacency list (at most 6 elements) makes the current approach efficient enough without the overhead of cache management and defensive copying.

      Parameters:
      format - The desired output format (Grid.ValueFormat.Index or Grid.ValueFormat.PackedInt).
      Returns:
      A short[] of adjacent cells to the first true cell, or null if no true cell exists.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if Grid.ValueFormat.Bitmask is used, as it is not suitable for representing individual cells.
      Since:
      2025.04 - First True Adjacents Method Creation
      See Also:
      • findAdjacents(short, ValueFormat)
      • findFirstTrueCell()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) operations and conversion due to fixed-size adjacency lists.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; depends on the result of non-thread-safe methods.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a new short[] for the result.

    • findFirstTrueAdjacents

      public short[] findFirstTrueAdjacents()
      Convenience overload for findFirstTrueAdjacents(ValueFormat) that assumes Grid.ValueFormat.Index for the output format.
      Returns:
      A short[] of adjacent cells to the first true cell in Grid.ValueFormat.Index format, or null if no true cell exists.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Format Support
      See Also:
      • findFirstTrueAdjacents(ValueFormat)
      Performance Characteristics:
      Delegates to the main implementation; O(1) complexity.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a new short[] for the result.

    • findFirstTrueAdjacentsAfter

      public short[] findFirstTrueAdjacentsAfter(short cell, Grid.ValueFormat inputFormat, Grid.ValueFormat outputFormat)
      Returns an array of adjacent cells to the first true cell that have an index greater than the specified cell.

      This method is a specialized pruning helper for the generator. It identifies potential subsequent clicks by filtering the adjacents of the first true cell, considering only those that appear after a given cell in the flattened grid order. This helps to establish bounds for combination generation.

      Algorithm Details

      The method first retrieves all adjacents of the first true cell using findFirstTrueAdjacents(ValueFormat). It then performs a binary search on this sorted list to find the first adjacent cell whose index is greater than the provided cell. A subarray containing only these subsequent adjacents is then returned, with optional format conversion.

      Performance Considerations

      The complexity is dominated by the binary search and array copy, resulting in an effectively constant time operation O(log(k) + m), where k is the number of adjacents (max 6) and m is the number of remaining adjacents to copy. Given the small size of k, this method is highly efficient. Caching the result is generally not beneficial due to the dynamic nature of the cell parameter and the small performance gains from avoiding an array copy.

      Parameters:
      cell - The reference cell (in inputFormat) after which to find adjacents.
      inputFormat - The Grid.ValueFormat of the input cell.
      outputFormat - The Grid.ValueFormat of the output adjacent cells.
      Returns:
      An array of adjacent cells that appear after the specified cell, or null if no true cell exists or no such adjacents are found.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if Grid.ValueFormat.Bitmask is used for any format.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Format Support
      See Also:
      • findAdjacents(short)
      • findFirstTrueAdjacents(ValueFormat)
      • findFirstTrueCell()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) effectively, due to small, fixed-size lists.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; relies on non-thread-safe methods.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a new short[] for the result.

    • isSolved

      public boolean isSolved()
      Checks if the grid is completely solved (i.e., all cells are false).

      This method determines if the puzzle is solved by checking if the getTrueCount() is zero. It leverages the lazy evaluation of trueCellsCount to ensure efficient and up-to-date checks without redundant calculations.

      Returns:
      true if the grid is solved (no true cells), false otherwise.
      Since:
      2025.03 - Initial Creation
      See Also:
      • recalculationNeeded
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) complexity.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; calls the non-thread-safe getTrueCount().
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • getTrueCount

      public int getTrueCount()
      Returns the count of true cells in the grid.

      This method implements a lazy evaluation strategy. If the grid state has been modified (indicated by recalculationNeeded), it recalculates the count using highly optimized bitwise operations (Long.bitCount(long)); otherwise, it returns the cached trueCellsCount.

      Returns:
      The number of true cells in the grid.
      Since:
      2025.03 - Dynamic True Cell Count Tracking
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) complexity.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe due to lazy evaluation of a mutable field.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • clone

      public Grid clone()
      Creates and returns a deep copy of this Grid instance.

      The cloned instance has its own independent copy of the grid state and cached values, ensuring that modifications to the clone do not affect the original. This method uses reflection to create a new instance of the same class, allowing for proper cloning of subclasses without requiring overrides in each subclass.

      Overrides:
      clone in class Object
      Returns:
      A deep copy of this Grid instance.
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - if the cloning process fails (e.g., due to reflection issues or an inaccessible constructor).
      Since:
      2025.03 - Cloning Introduction
      See Also:
      • firstTrueCell
      • gridState
      • recalculationNeeded
      • trueCellsCount
      • Object.clone()
      • Cloneable
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) complexity (fixed number of field copies).
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe, as it returns a new, independent instance.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a new Grid object and a new long[2] for its state.

    • canAffectFirstTrueCell

      public static boolean canAffectFirstTrueCell(short firstTrueCell, short clickCell, Grid.ValueFormat format)
      Determines if a prospective click on a clickCell can affect or create a new first true cell in the grid.

      This method is a crucial pruning helper for the generator. Since any valid solution must ultimately toggle the puzzle's first true cell, combinations whose initial clicks cannot influence this state can be discarded early.

      Scenarios Considered:

      • If there are no true cells (firstTrueCell == -1), any click can create one, so it returns true.
      • If the clickCell is at or before the firstTrueCell in flattened grid order, it is considered capable of affecting it, returning true.
      • If the clickCell is adjacent to the firstTrueCell, it can directly affect its state, returning true.
      If none of these conditions are met, the click cannot affect or create a new firstTrueCell, and the method returns false.
      Parameters:
      firstTrueCell - The current first true cell in the specified format.
      clickCell - The cell being considered for a click, in the specified format.
      format - The Grid.ValueFormat of both firstTrueCell and clickCell.
      Returns:
      true if the click can affect or create a new first true cell, false otherwise.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if Grid.ValueFormat.Bitmask is used for any format.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Format and Adjacency Optimizations
      See Also:
      • areAdjacent(short, short, ValueFormat)
      • findFirstTrueCell(ValueFormat)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) complexity due to fixed-size adjacency lookups and binary search on small arrays.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; relies only on immutable static data and input parameters.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • areAdjacent

      public static boolean areAdjacent(short cellA, short cellB, Grid.ValueFormat format)
      Determines if two cells are adjacent to each other in the grid.

      This method leverages the pre-computed ADJACENCY_CACHE for O(1) lookups, providing a highly efficient way to check adjacency. Adjacency is critical for the puzzle's mechanics, as clicks only affect neighboring cells.

      Performance Considerations

      The core lookup in ADJACENCY_CACHE is extremely fast. The primary performance consideration is the overhead of format conversion if Grid.ValueFormat.PackedInt is used. For optimal performance in hot paths, it is recommended to use Grid.ValueFormat.Index directly.

      Parameters:
      cellA - The first cell to check.
      cellB - The second cell to check.
      format - The Grid.ValueFormat of both input cells (Grid.ValueFormat.Index or Grid.ValueFormat.PackedInt).
      Returns:
      true if the cells are adjacent, false otherwise.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if Grid.ValueFormat.Bitmask is used for any format.
      ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException - if either cell is out of bounds.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Index Format Usage
      See Also:
      • areAdjacent(short, short)
      • canAffectFirstTrueCell(short, short, ValueFormat)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) lookup in the cache, plus conversion overhead if applicable.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; accesses immutable, pre-computed static data.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • areAdjacent

      public static boolean areAdjacent(short cellA, short cellB)
      Convenience overload for areAdjacent(short, short, ValueFormat) that assumes Grid.ValueFormat.Index for both input cells.
      Parameters:
      cellA - The first cell to check, in Grid.ValueFormat.Index format.
      cellB - The second cell to check, in Grid.ValueFormat.Index format.
      Returns:
      true if the cells are adjacent, false otherwise.
      Throws:
      ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException - if either cell is out of bounds.
      Since:
      2025.06 - Odd Adjacency Pruning
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) lookup.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • getGridState

      public long[] getGridState()
      Returns a copy of the current grid state as a long[2] bitmask.

      This method provides direct access to a snapshot of the grid's internal state. While it involves an allocation, it is useful for debugging, logging, or scenarios requiring immutable copies of the grid state without exposing the internal array directly.

      Returns:
      A copy of the current grid state as a long[2] bitmask.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Bitmasked Grid State
      See Also:
      • click(long[])
      • Grid.ValueFormat.Bitmask
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) complexity (fixed-size array copy).
      Threading Model:
      The returned array is thread-safe as it is a new, independent copy.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a new long[2] array.

    • printGrid

      public void printGrid()
      Prints the current grid state to the logger in a human-readable format.

      Cells are represented by '1' for true (on) and '0' for false (off). Rows are indented to visually reflect the hexagonal layout, matching the format used in the original PDC puzzle description. This method is primarily for debugging and visualization.

      Since:
      2025.03 - Grid Printing Method Creation
      See Also:
      • EVEN_NUM_COLS
      • NUM_ROWS
      • ODD_NUM_COLS
      • getBit(int)
      • indexToPacked(short)
      • packedToIndex(short)
      • Grid.ValueFormat.Index
      • Grid.ValueFormat.PackedInt
      • StringBuilder
      • Logger
      • LogManager
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(NUM_CELLS) due to iteration over all cells. Not performance-critical.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; iterates over the mutable grid state.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a new StringBuilder for each row.

Performance-optimized brute-force solver for hexagonal grid Lights Out puzzles
